methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
Valinor Enterprises is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Trae Stephens and Grant Verstandig. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) in the defense sector, including direct comparables Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $85M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $170M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations of $500M, $300M, and $150M respectively, establishing a median of $300M and range of $150M–$500M.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $144M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $235M. The dispersion between methods reflects material uncertainty around comparable selection and stage-progression benchmarks in the defense sector. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for this sector and founder profile, and whether the three public and sampled private comparables adequately represent Valinor's growth trajectory and risk profile. The peer-median approach weights more heavily given its grounding in direct market transactions.
